entry.title: Re: Is Search String for Electronic Bank Statement still...
entry.content:
Hi Vanessa,

then you have to adjust the REGEX function and add whatever prefix could be there. If you have issues to create such a REGEX, please use LLM models out there (ChatGPT etc.) to generate a REGEX function for you. It's really not that hard. The algorithm 421 should be fine.

 

Best, Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: Source of data in data cluster
entry.content:
Hello @VISHAL_JHA 

As per SAP Help documentation to IMPORT FROM DATABASE

If DATABASE is specified, the data cluster that was written to the export/import table dbtab in the area ar and under the ID specified in id

 Therefore you data sits in ZCL_REPDB data cluster.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Analytical Query on SAP Public Cloud: Parameter value...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can refer to the following blog, on how to add value help for parameter in ABAP CDS Analytical query.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-add-value-help-for-parameter-in-abap-cds-analytical-query/ba-p/13377436

entry.title: Re: Auto Flow - scheduled feature
entry.content: Hi Peter, you need to use the option "Scheduled" when creating the autoflow. Simon

entry.title: Consuming SAP Datasphere business content in different spaces in a single tier environment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I've discovered that when exporting an analytical model in SAP Datasphere, the JSON export includes all the dependent objects such as tables and views. This means you can import the JSON file into a different space along with the analytical model and all its dependencies.

 

With this in mind, I'm wondering if it's possible to install business content using the JSON/CSN export and import method in different spaces (such as Dev Space, QA Space, and Prod Space) within a single-tenant environment. 

 

I have a requirement to consume sales business content in two spaces (DEV and Prod) in a single tier SAP Datasphere environment. The DEV space will have a connection to S4 On-Prem and Prod a connection to S4 On-Prem. I'd appreciate any thoughts and comments on this. Thanks!

entry.title: Synchronize, Check Out, Update & Load
entry.content: May I know what is the differences between Synchronize Workarea, Check Out Objects, Update from Server and Load from Server?

entry.title: Re: Sales Cloud V2 Questions - Integration, Embedded SAC,...
entry.content:
Hi,

1 = you should be able to this with Validations and Determinations on the business object.

3 = Yes, you can create extesion fields and use them in SAC Embedded.

KR, Simon

entry.title: Re: Source of data in data cluster
entry.content: You don't say what inconsistency is detected. Read the ABAP documentation, it explains. My 2 cents that ZCL_REPDB is using the oldest format "Data Clusters in Multiple Rows". My 2 cents that your error happens after a system upgrade. See "The release-dependent internal format of data clusters can be converted." (https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abencl_abap_expimp.htm)

entry.title: I'm not able to create trial tenant in SAP BTP Trial account
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm trying to create a trial tenant in SAP BTP Trial account by following this link 
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/get-your-tenant?locale=en-US#get-trial-tenant 

But I'm getting this error 

Tried doing it from the Command line as well but same error 

I need to perform this tutorial 
https://github.wdf.sap.corp/CPSecurity/Knowledge-Base/tree/master/08_Tutorials/iasbroker#registration 

Any help is appreciated 
Thanks in Advance

entry.title: Re: Source of data in data cluster
entry.content: The problem is there is no Export statement is getting executed through out the call and when I am opening it in SE11 the table content icon is disabled.

entry.title: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly combined
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

At the moment DM is presenting us with a small challenge.

We have developed a custom view plugin that we want to use not just once in the POD, but twice. The plugin displays data that is read from the custom fields and is controlled with configuration options.

Since it is not allowed in the POD Designer to assign an app twice, we have tried to create a second deployment using various settings in order to get an almost identical second plugin into the POD. But a new name and a new namespace do not seem to be enough.

The problem now is that it is apparently not entirely clear when a plugin counts as a separate plugin.

We have tried the following and failed. DM recognizes both plugins in the POD Designer, but ultimately acts as if they were the same, although the name, the namespace, the folder structure, the deployment URL and the entry in the “Manage Service Registry” are different.

Question:
How does DM recognize a difference between the plugins?
Where is the limit, when the DM recognizes a plugin as independent?
At the moment it seems that similarly named plugins like "peggingGraph" & "peggingGraph2" are not possible.


Appendix:

POD Designer shows properties of one plugin, although the second is different.
When we try to drag and drop it in the POD, we get the standard error message "Plugin already in use".

Manage Service Registry different:

Deployed are two different services:

 

 

SAP Digital Manufacturing 
@ManoelCostaif possible, please refer to a developer, thanks

entry.title: Re: Error during request to remote service: Failed to loa...
entry.content: Have a look at this blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/split-mta-into-backend-frontend-the-final-piece/ba-p/13510109 and this one https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/split-mta-into-backend-frontend-standalone-approuter/ba-p/13511910

entry.title: Best practice - SAP Public Cloud - settings for Information Lifecycle Management (1KA) Settings
entry.content:
I am currently working on a project that involves configuring the settings for Information Lifecycle Management (1KA). Given the critical nature of these settings, I wanted to reach out to see if you or anyone in your network has any experience or detailed insights with this specific scope item.

As the SAP Standard settings are empty I'm searching for best practices for the SAP FI objects in SAP Public Cloud. 

I wanted to ask if we have any best practice settings or configuration in the area of finance objects a previous customer project that could serve as a reference. This would be extremely helpful and save valuable time. Any advice, best practices, or resources you could share regarding Information Lifecycle Management (1KA) would be immensely helpful and greatly appreciated!!

entry.title: Form Template for Supplier Invoice which Entry Type = [Online] and [Invoice from Parking Function]
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Supplier Invoice with Entry Type=ESR have output tab and we can view the form template for those Supplier Invoices.

 

But supplier invoice with other Entry Type= Online, Invoice from Parking function and etc.. do not have output tab and we cannot view the form template. Only ESR type can view form template.

Could you please guide us how to view the form template for supplier invoices with other entry types?

#Supplier Invoice template, #template for Entry Type Online, #template for SIV entry type

Varun

entry.title: HANA Public Cloud validation _Precondition field/ function ( SYST-BATCH <> 'X AND SYST-BINPT <> )
entry.content:
Hello All,

Our client requires us to create a validation in the HANA Public Cloud based on the precondition field/function (SYST-BATCH <> 'X' AND SYST-BINPT <> 'X'). However, we could not find this field/function in the HANA Public Cloud.

Please assist us in understanding how we can achieve this validation in the HANA Public Cloud using the Manage Substitution/Validation app."

 

Thank you 

JP

entry.title: Re: Action [core] page-editor/create-custom-section faile...
entry.content: Hi @Sanya_Govil it may be an issue with your particular project, we would need to have a look at it. You can create an archive of the project via the "Archive project" function from the right-click menu of the project folder. This will create a zip. Place it on a file share like onedrive and send us a link to it via email to SAPFioriElements@sap.com (direct attached zips may be filtered out). Alternatively you can create a support case with SAP on the SAP Fiori tools component CA-UX-IDE, and let us know the number once submitted.

entry.title: Custom Key Figure in Percentage doesn´t appear in KPI on startpage
entry.content:
Hello,

I created a custom calculated key figure "Project Win Value". The idea is to show all "Won Opportunities" in a percentage figures in a KPI on startpage. 

KPI on startpage but wrong value from Key figure

In my KPI on startpage appears "0". In my created report there I can see the right key figures value in Percentage. In my example "41 %". 

 

Calculated Key Figure

Where is the mistake that I can´t show the Project Win Value 41% on start page?

 

Thanks & BR
Lishede

entry.title: Re: How to Customize SAC UI Themes: A Basic Guide
entry.content: This is great! I'm currently working on some SAC story pages for my product and these tips were truly helpful! Thank you! 🙂

entry.title: Re: NEW Substance Volume Tracking - S/4HANA for Product c...
entry.content:
Hello Shashi,

yes we would recommend to switch to the standard compliance requirement when it becomes available. One of the reasons is, that the compliance requirements which are part of the embedded content are being updated on a regular basis. This means e.g. that you get substance list updates automatically via the content updates.
The transition from the customer-specific compliance requirement to the standard compliance requirement would look as follows: 
You disable the customer-specific compliance requirement in the App Enable Substance Volume Tracking - for Compliance Requirements. This will stop the tracking process for this compliance requirement.
Then you have to enable the standard compliance requirement in the same app. When you enable a new compliance requirement for Substance Volume Tracking, the system will automatically start an initial tracking for the current tracking period. This means, there is no need to transfer any already tracked substance volumes which were tracked for the customer-specific compliance requirement to the standard compliance requirement.

Best regards,
Michael

entry.title: Re: Posting Change with RF
entry.content:
Hello,
Can you give us the process flow how to do it 
Or some screenshot 
Regards,

entry.title: Re: Tax entered incorrect (code 5A, amount 0), correct 113 YEN. Message no. FF7
entry.content: Breakdown into installment payments not possible for item 001

entry.title: Re: mass-change Business Partners
entry.content:
Hi Haily,

If you are using s/4hana cloud public edition (not sure as the tag was removed) you can also use "Manage Mass Maintenance - Business Partners" (F1047A) (this seems to be the superordinate app for "Start Mass Maintenance - Business Partners").

Here you can click Start to create a new process.

Either way, you will need to create an import file. I think the easiest way to do this is to go to 'Export Master Data - Business Partners' (F3251) and export the table "BUT100 - Roles".

The first two rows in the exported table (Excel file) are hidden. The action code column must be visible for the upload to work. It is important to add the action code "I" for insert (capitalisation required). A 'Valid_From' and 'Valid_To' date is also required.

Here is an example adding the role FLCU01 to existing business partners. You may also need FLCU00, which is the Customer Finance role.

Best Regards
Simon

entry.title: Re: Hierarchies in dropdowns
entry.content:
SAP Managed Tags: SAP Analytics Cloud, analytics designer

 

Hi community

I am new to SAC.b I want to introduce a dropdown filtering menu in my story. I succeded in populating the dropdown using this lines of code in the onInitialization()


var Inspectors_list = Table_1.getDataSource().getMembers("Don_ordre.Inspecteur");

if (Inspectors_list.length > 0) 
{
for (var i=0;i<Inspectors_list.length; i++){
Dropdown_1.addItem(Inspectors_list[i].id,Inspectors_list[i].description);
}}
 
the i proceeded to to adding the following lines of code in the Dropdown_1 - onSelect()

var x = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
console.log(x);
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Don_ordre.Inspecteur",x);


The filter is not working correctly and i do not understand why.
There is only two values in the dropdown once selected returns values , all the others returns zero.

entry.title: What is happen if change 'Page Size' 100 to 500 in 'Configure the OData V2 Receiver Adapter'
entry.content:
As far as I can see from the SAP help portal (
Please check the attached image and Configure the OData V2 Receiver Adapter | SAP Help Portal)

, 'Page Size' seems to change the number of records to be read at one time. But if this value is changed to 100→500, Besides performance improvements, what other impact does this have on the environment? I would appreciate it if you could see both the advantages and disadvantages.

Sorry , I would appreciate it if you could see both the advantages and disadvantages.

entry.content:
Hi All,

There is a requirement to populate compa ratios of employees in reports. Saw that its not possible via table reports. Has anyone come across this requirement and has a solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Number Range Objects for MM Master data and transnational data
entry.content: excellent summary document on number ranges. it is very usefull

entry.title: Dropdown filtering in SAC
entry.content:
SAP Managed Tags: SAP Analytics Cloud, analytics designer

Hi community

I am new to SAC.

I want to introduce a dropdown filtering menu in my story. I succeded in populating the dropdown using these lines of code in the onInitialization() function : 


var Inspectors_list = Table_1.getDataSource().getMembers("Don_ordre.Inspecteur");

if (Inspectors_list.length > 0) 
{
for (var i=0;i<Inspectors_list.length; i++){
Dropdown_1.addItem(Inspectors_list[i].id,Inspectors_list[i].description);
}}
 
Then i proceeded to to adding the following lines of code in the Dropdown_1 - onSelect() :

var x = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
console.log(x);
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Don_ordre.Inspecteur",x);


The filter is not working correctly and i do not understand why.
There is only two values in the dropdown once selected returns values , all the others returns zero.

Also i figured if it's an issue with my model , why would two values from the dropdown filter correctly and the others won't.

 

 

Also some screenshots from the modeler

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Rotation of service keys and bindings due to Certificate CA expiration
entry.content:
Hi @Josue_Cruz 

If you are a user of PostgreSQL on SAP BTP, yes the procedure is identical for AWS and Azure because SAP takes care of including in the service keys the new certificates, you just need to recreate the service keys and bindings. If you are referring to the direct use of native AWS service via your own managed account then you need to follow https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/rotate-your-ssl-tls-certificates-now-amazon-rds-and-amazon-aurora-expire-in-2024/ .

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previo...
entry.content: In Q2: I am not sure what is in Measure A, Measure B, and Measure C, but those likely should be in a Standard Account dimension so that you can use Account formulas. It seems like you may be trying to do row-based calculations in Measures, but Measures in SAC are meant for Column-based calculations. I don't know your full use case.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello, experts
I have a question about Build Apps. I understand that the build app is based on Low code/No code
Can't you draw a screen using codes such as ABAP or JAVASCRIPT at all? I wonder if I can draw a screen by directly inserting the code rather than calling it through OData. For example, I drag and use a button with drag and drop, but I wonder if it is possible to draw a button on the screen by squeezing it into code at the same time
Rather than writing free JavaScript code like in a typical coding environment, it can be used in a limited environment. For example, processing a specific variable or adding a simple calculation logic, is that right?

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408/SolP/1YI?region=DE

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
Re: Sales order specific production not allowing to GRN i...
entry.content: Check this discussion

entry.title: Re: Changelog deletion with Process chain does not work
entry.content:
According the screenshot there are outgoing deltas from this ADSO (Tgt flag).

If its white, it means, that the request was not updated to any delta targets at all.

If its green it means, that the requests were updated only to a subset of delta targets.

All requests must have a "checkered" flag so you can delete the CL data.

This was exactly that I stated in my very first answer, yet you insisted that the ADSO has no delta targets. According the screenshot it definitely has.

 

Did you also checked the KBA, that I mentioned?

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previo...
entry.content: Yes, we are trying to do row-based calculation so called "Cross Calculation" in Optimized design experience in SAC. A/B/C measures are key figures representing different kind of KPIs. Customer requested the format in this way so we are figuring out if it's possible or not

entry.title: Re: How to export CSV file with standard and custom API S...
entry.content:
Hi,

1) The S/4HC standard APIs only support JSON/XML formats. One option to consider therefore would be to use the Integration Suite (a.k.a CPI) as a middleware where you can have a mapping logic to convert the XML structure to a CSV file (see an example here) 

2) The only possibility to create a "custom" APi would be a custom CDS View published as an external API. However this custom API only supports an XML format as well, so in this case, you would also need a middleware to carry out an XML to CSV mapping.

Best regards
Varun

Roberto

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA - Unsupported Media Type
entry.content:
Hi

Most probably you are using an outdated BW Modeling Tools Version. Check the KBA:
3460242 ADSO cannot be created due to error "Unsupported Media Type"

and update it to the most recent version together with the ADT.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Post Asset Expense

entry.title: adjust production order qty to reflect delivery tolerance
entry.content:
Hi MRP Experts,

We are operating in an MTO environment, where our customers requesting us always to ship the full order qty with a tolerance of -0%/+10%. Unfortunatly MRP is triggering the nominal production order qty to the nominal sales order qty and component scrap of the BOM is in 30% of the cases not sufficient to achieve nominal sales order qty.

I do not wnat to add mor scrap to the BOM but would rather prefer to set the nominal production order qty on the upper end of the sales order tolerance to ensure I maximize sales and do not bother the customer with shorts.

What is the best way to achieve such?

Thx

Matt

entry.title: Re: Oci: How handel Cancel button?
entry.content: As mentioned, I want to integrate this button into my OCI catalog. There are instances where users may want to navigate back to the SRM without selecting any products. I would like to know how I can send a response back to the Hook_URL, including a message or any additional parameters.

entry.title: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Many of you are certainly already familiar with the baseline concept in the SAPGUI-based ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC). Baseline offers you the possibility to exclude the well-known or legacy ATC findings from the ATC result to focus your quality assurance efforts only on the new or recently changed code. See also the blog post Remote Code Analysis in ATC - Working with Baseline to suppress findings in old legacy code.

With SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2408 (August 2024 release) we have enabled the baseline also for ATC on SAP BTP for checking of your private cloud (or on-premise) developments. The overview of the central ATC quality system based on SAP BTP ABAP Environment can be found in the blog post Usage of ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud for on-premise developments. Please note that, in order to use the baseline, you also need the Quality Manager – Software Development business role.

Baseline can be created in the Custom Code Migration app. After execution of the ATC run by the custom code analysis project just use the button Add Findings to Baseline to take all ATC findings of this ATC run into the baseline. Thereby you have different options how to proceed with the ATC findings:

  • Exempt Findings will indicate the findings as exempted (our recommendation)
  • Suppress Findings will exclude findings from the ATC result list.
  • Reduce Findings‘ Priority will assign the low priority 3 (Information) to the findings

Let’s check out how to work with baseline based on a couple of examples.

Example 1: Take over all findings into baseline as exempted

After execution of the ATC check run by the custom code analysis project use the button Add Findings to Baseline and choose the option …to Exempt Findings:

After this you can display the status of the custom code analysis project with regard to the baseline in the projects overview by adding the column Findings Added To Baseline. All findings in the baseline are exempted now:

After a renewed ATC check run (create the new custom code analysis project with the same parameters), all findings that are included in the related ATC check result will be treated as exempted and will not appear in the custom code analysis project result:

If developers run ATC checks during development, the findings of the baseline will also not appear in their ATC results. You can remove the findings of the ATC check run from the baseline using the respective custom code analysis project and the Remove Findings From Baseline button:

Example 2: Assign the lower priority to the findings and developer perspective

Now imagine you as developer in your on-premise (or private cloud) system use the central ATC on BTP (see also the blog post How to use ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud as a developer in an on-premise system).

After execution of the ATC check run for cloud readiness in your ABAP development tools for Eclipse you get the ATC error about the usage of the API that will not be released:  

You want to take care of this ATC error later and to do so you can take it into the baseline and reduce the priority. After execution of the ATC run by the custom code analysis project you will see this ATC error in the custom code analysis project result:

On the project view just use the button Add Findings to Baseline and choose the option …to Reduce Findings‘ Priority:

After a renewed ATC check run (create the new custom code analysis project with the same parameters), you will get for this ATC finding information message instead of error in the custom code analysis project result:

If you now as developer in your ABAP development tools for Eclipse recheck your source code with ATC you will also get the information message instead of the error for this finding in your ATC result:

NOTE: please consider that ATC baseline is effective as long as the related code sections remain unchanged. If the code is changed or a new code is added, the unique identifier for each ATC finding changes so that it does not match any more with the finding that has been added to the baseline. Therefore you will get the findings again if you add new code lines to your source code.

Please be aware that after you added the findings of the custom code analysis project to the baseline this project can not be deleted. Only after removing the findings of the project from the baseline you can delete this project.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment
entry.content:
Hi @moeez 

The public opensap environment has been discontinued, you need to bring your own SAP Business One installation or use SAP CAL and expose the service layer to the internet to use SAP Build Apps.

Best regards,
Darius

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus-Pune (August 2024)
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Community in Pune & Mumbai! We have some exciting news to share with you!


We are thrilled to announce that the SAP Community events will be making a comeback in Pune & Mumbai, starting with the SAP Stammtisch Plus on August 30th, 2024.

This event is an excellent opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and anyone interested in learning more about SAP technologies to come together and learn, network with like-minded individuals, and have fun.

Are you wondering what an SAP Stammtisch Plus event is?

SAP Stammtisch Plus is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP-related topics. The event's primary focus is networking & discussions, with 2 or 3 sessions from community members.

Learn more about it at: SAP Stammtisch - what is it about?

What is in it for you?

Let’s cut to the chase, what will you get by attending or contributing to this event?

1. Networking – You get to meet like-minded tech folks and have more space for networking.
2. Learning     – You will learn about the latest SAP technologies from experts.


 

 

 

 

When?
30th August 2024 from 2:00 PM till 4:00 PM. Registration starts at 1:30 PM.

Where?
3rd Floor, Wing 2 Block 1, International Tech Park, Wagholi Village, Kharadi Road, Pune - 411014. Phone: + 91 20 41985101

Registration

Please register using the below link. - SAP Stammtisch Plus (konfhub.com)

Note: We have only limited seats available, so register ONLY if you plan to attend IN-PERSON on August 30, Friday in Pune.

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Pari1 
  • @Asutosh21 
  • @Shweta_Kadam 
  • @Nitin_Rakh 
  • @SmitaP1 
  • @ranjeetkumar07 
  • @Devkambli 


We hope to see you all there and look forward to an engaging and productive event. Don't forget to register using the link provided above to secure your spot. See you soon!

Best Regards
SAP Community Group – Pune

entry.title: Join Us Live: SAP Customer Data Cloud & Commerce Cloud Integration Essentials
entry.content:
What is it about?

In this live session, you will explore the integration between SAP Customer Data Cloud and SAP Commerce Cloud.

Learning objectives

By the end of this live session, you will be able to:

  • Configure the integration between SAP Customer Data Cloud, SAP Commerce Cloud, and Composable Storefront
  • Navigate through the integration codes and setup configurations
  • Validate the integration flows and features within SAP Customer Data Cloud, SAP Commerce Cloud, and Composable Storefront

Target Audience 

  • Technical Consultants
  • Functional Consultants

How can you book it?


This live session is available on SAP Learning Hub. You will need to have a subscription.
You can click here to register for this session.

Course Details


Date: 18 October 2024

Time: From 09:00 to 10:30 (GMT +2)

Please Note

For more information on this integration topic, check out this blog post:

SAP Customer Data Cloud Integration with Commerce Cloud and Composable Storefront Blog 


For a better understanding of the live session’s content, it’s recommended to also attend the:

    • SAP Customer Data Cloud videos

 

    • SAP Customer Data Cloud C4H620 ILT training

 

    • Exploring SAP Customer Data Cloud Essentials eLearning



before or after attending the live session.

If you want to know more about this training, or if you want to get informed on any updates of this live session, please follow this blog post by clicking on the “follow” green button on the left.

If you have any questions, feel free to add them as a comment to this post.

entry.title: Registrations Now Open for SAP Inside Track Gurugram 2024 - Business AI Edition
entry.content:
We are excited to extend an invitation to SAP Inside Track (SIT) Gurugram on September 14, 2024!

In this year SIT, we present a distinctive opportunity focused on SAP Business AI – the forefront of innovation and the arena where the future is taking form.

Come join us as we dive into the latest advancements and discover how AI is transforming business operations. Our lineup includes thought leaders, industry experts, and hands-on workshops designed to equip you with the knowledge and skills to harness the power of AI. Whether you are a seasoned professional or just beginning your journey, this event promises to inspire and empower you to drive meaningful change. Networking with peers, exchange of insights, and discovery of new tools and strategies will set you apart in the rapidly evolving digital landscape.

This free event is your gateway to unlocking the full potential of SAP and AI in your organization. 

What is SAP Inside Track? 
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!


Event Details:  

Date: September 14, 2024
Time: 9 AM - 5 PM IST 
Venue: SAP Labs India, Golf Course Rd, Suncity, Sector 54, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

 Note: This is an in-person event only.

Agenda:  

This SAP Inside Track will be one-full-day exploration of SAP Business AI. You will: 

  • Gain expertise in developing AI-powered applications on the SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • Explore innovative Business AI solutions provided by our esteemed partners.
  • Experience the impactful potential of Embedded AI through interactive demonstrations featuring various SAP solutions.
  • Our upcoming event will feature a wide range of sessions led by SAP experts, partners, and customers.
  • Attendees will also have an opportunity to engage in hands-on workshops led by our AI specialists in a designated area. Additionally, demo booths will showcase AI applications and solutions.

The event agenda is currently being crafted, with a separate blog post set to be released soon. Stay informed by joining the SAP Community Gurugram group!

Speaker Registration:  

Registrations for speakers are now open! If your expertise lies in building AI applications on SAP BTP, creating innovative Business AI solutions, or sharing your experience with Embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA and SuccessFactors, we welcome your contributions. We also welcome our Partners and Customers to join us in sharing their expertise and experience in Business AI and showcasing the stellar work to the entire community.

Register now if you want to be a speaker! Stay tuned for an upcoming blog post that will include track details and a link to submit your topic and speaker information.

Exclusive Sponsorship: 

With a record footfall of 4000+ community members across locations in last 12 months we are humbled to serve the ever-growing appetite of the community to Learn, Network and Grow. If you wish to join us in contributing to the community and want to have access as a sponsor, please drop in a comment below and we will reach out to you! This time we have exclusively bundled offerings to ensure that the sponsors get to not only showcase their content but also showcase their expertise to the entire community across locations including Bangalore, Gurugram, Hyderabad and Pune. 

Join the Gurugram SAP Community Group:  

If you live in Gurgaon, Delhi, Noida, or nearby areas, we invite you to join our vibrant Gurugram SAP Community Group. Engage in meaningful discussions, share ideas, and collaborate with fellow SAP enthusiasts.

Also, follow the Linkedin and Twitter #sitGGN for SAP Inside Track Gurgaon and for all Gurgaon/NCR community events updates or to share your ideas.

Best regards,

Jitan on behalf of SAP Community Gurugram

entry.title: Re: kate AMQP channel in error in SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Ramu.

 

In the error screenshot "readAddress(..)" failed is shown.

The adapter is failing to read from IP address which is mentioned in   readAddress("IP Address").  

This is third party application issue.

Please try to ping this IP address from PI server and restart the sender adapter.

Appears the third party server may not be temporarily available.

Please inform inform third party asap.

Regards

Anupam

entry.title: Power Designer Repository - SQL QUERY and repository tables
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a SQL script to retrieve information of all the tables that were created in power designer from the repository.

That is for example to retrieve information such as under which schema is a certain table in and how many attributes it has, how many primary keys, does certain attributes of the entities have the RGDP flag.

Basicly I want to get a detailed list of the tables created in PwD for its repository.

I have identified certain repository tables that have some of these information such PMTEXT, PMOBJT, PMRLSH, PMCLSS.

What other repository tables can I use, I am not able to find any good documentations on this and with even the tables I have identified I don't know what some of their columns are used for.

Can someone point me in the right direction for some documentation or give me a list of repository table with some explanations.

 

Thank you in advancee for your help.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5: Can a Smartfield be used in place of an Input?
entry.content:
Hi Adam,
Were you able to solve your problem?
I'm in the same situation now and would be grateful if you could share your solution idea.

Did you also consider providing any easy way to inform the end users which format the upload file should be in (or an empty template for them to download)? Otherwise, end users will be going through a lot of trial and error, (or modelers have to vigorously update end user instructions somewhere).

entry.content:
During employee creation  in Manage Workforce app, telephone details and Mobile details both are optional.

Is it possible to make any one of them mandatory during employee creation ?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Failed to connect to sbocommon on installation
entry.content:
i tried everything from checking the links, checking the sld, and many other things. telnet to the database ip and host returns true. been stuck for 3 days, i have deadline to chase

 SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA

entry.title: Re: Telephone or mobile details optional during employee...
entry.content:
There is an SCCUI 103515  for validating Email address field as optional or mandatory.

But Telephone number or mobile number is always optional.

entry.title: Re: One of the database Schema Export failed from SLD
entry.content:
Dear Team

This issue is resolve for me by doing the below steps.

 

1-) Login to SLD server SUSE

2-) Goto /var/log/SAPBusinessOne/BackupService/logs/NDB_192.168.64.14_30013

3-) in the above directory find the database.pid file and edit and clear all pid and save check again.

 

Regards,

Maaz

entry.title: Re: Source of data in data cluster
entry.content: ZCL_REPDB is a custom table. Use SE11 > "Where-Used List" to identify what your custom programs store in this table. Only you can answer. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Setup of the SAP Signavio Process Insights connector within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

@lukas_stier

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - How to estimate the remaining storage free space?
entry.content:
Hi,

For anyone who may be interested in monitoring instances of PostgreSQL we have now include a reference to the official documentation about the new monitoring APIs. Find below the update:

"

If you want to monitor the consumption of resources of your instance, including the % of storage space at any moment, you can use the below extension APIs:

Retrieve the instance status and monitoring metrics:

  • /postgresql-db/instances/:id/status
  • /postgresql-db/instances/:id/metrics

The above extension APIs allows to retrieve status of an instance and the below metrics:

  • CPU Utilization (%)
  • Memory Utilization (%)
  • Disk Utilization (%)
  • Max Connections (%)

For more details on how to use the above APIs please check the product SAP Help Portal official documentation:

  • Extension API Reference: Instance Monitoring 

"

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hi,

Completed the task 2 with concatenated value of Total_Price and Currency_Code and Overall Status Text.

entry.title: Re: Java Cap remote debugging not working with Java 17
entry.content:
Hello, I am coming back to add our solution, sorry for being late.

The comments helped a lot, we had to find out the process ID and then it actually works:

pid=$(cf ssh appName -c "ps -C java -o pid=") && cf ssh appName -c "~/app/META-INF/.sap_java_buildpack/sap_machine_jdk/bin/jcmd $pid VM.start_java_debugging" && cf ssh appName -N -T -L 8000:127.0.0.1:8000

Thank you for all of your help.

entry.title: Re: Consume CDS CAP oData Service by Workzone UI Integrat...
entry.content:
@ckuczera 

I assume that your XSUAA instance is using binding secret.

"oauth2-configuration": {
    "credential-types": ["binding-secret"]
}

With the above setting, a new secret is generated every time the app is deployed. If you want to fix the secret, you can switch to instance secret.

"oauth2-configuration": {
    "credential-types": ["binding-secret","instance-secret"]
}

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/service-instance-secrets

entry.title: how to assign the FICO Form in the place of standard form
entry.content:
how to assign customised sapscript form in the place of standard one for cash journal in FICO.... can anybody give me the exact solution I tried to assigned it in ob96 and then fbcjc3 but the output in fbcj is standard form only....

can anybody give me the solution for this problem

entry.title: Re: How to send notification (Success) after an object is...
entry.content:
They're just telling you not to use that deprecated interface in your code. Use the one from the `com.hybris.cockpitng` package instead. The cockpitng implementation extends the deprecated backoffice one so the methods are available to you use.

At some point in the future they'll remove the interface and implementation from the `com.hybris.backoffice` package and copy the methods to the new interface instead and because you've already switched over it'll all happen seamlessly🤞. If you don't change the use of the deprecated interface now then when it is removed you'll get compilation failures and have to switch to the interface in the updated package then instead.

entry.title: Re: Dunning process
entry.content:
Hello, Usually the items which incoming payment methods defined are not dunned, as this implies that the onus of collecting/initiating the payment is with the receiver and the payer. In case you want to define the payment methods relevant for dunning, then these items will also be considered for dunning. But please note that we should not include such items for payment run as you cannot dun as well as initiate the payment at the same time.

For the second part, of your question: we define the payment methods applicable for a specific country first and then we can use only these payment methods in the company code level setting. Important thing here to note is that while running APP, we can pay only for the countries that are in the same country. Company codes from different countries cannot participate in a particular payment run.

entry.title: OIDC vs. SAML your hybrid SAP Landscape: What You Need to Know
entry.content:
If you're a security enthusiast, you're probably already familiar with SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language). It's a reliable and relatively straightforward protocol for enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) in client applications. However, with more and more SAP applications supporting OIDC (OpenID Connect) for SSO, you might find yourself wondering which protocol to choose, especially if you're embarking on a new SSO journey.

Short Answer: Each option has its pros and cons, and if your application supports both, it shouldn't significantly affect user experience.

Long Answer: Keep reading to delve into the nuances and differences between these two protocols.

 

Characteristics of SAML and OIDC

Both SAML and OIDC play crucial roles in SSO implementation. While they offer similar end-user experiences, they differ significantly in how they are implemented and managed by developers. Here's a closer look:

SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language)

  • Format: Uses XML-based messages to securely exchange authentication and authorization data.
  • Interoperability: Widely adopted by a variety of commercial and open-source identity providers and service providers.
  • Protocol Complexity: Generally complex to implement, involving verbose and cumbersome XML files.
  • Attributes: Customizable attribute profiles for fine-grained access control and identity management.
  • Security: Strong support for mechanisms like digital signatures and encryption.
  • Use Case: Typically used in enterprises, educational institutions, and industries where detailed attribute-based access control is necessary.

OIDC (OpenID Connect)

  • Format: Uses JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and RESTful APIs, making the data transmission lighter and less verbose compared to XML.
  • Interoperability: Rapidly adopted across many platforms and services, often integrated with OAuth 2.0 for authorization.
  • Protocol Complexity: Easier to implement with modern web technologies due to its JSON and REST-oriented architecture.
  • Attributes: Relies on standardized scopes and claims to manage user information.
  • Security: Secure with modern cryptographic algorithms and supports features like token revocation and refresh tokens.
  • Use Case: Commonly used in consumer-facing applications, social logins, and scenarios where simplicity and speed are crucial, such as mobile applications and Single Page Applications (SPA).

 

Key Similarities and Differences

Here's a quick overview of the characteristics, similarities, and differences between SAML and OIDC:

 

Characteristic

SAML

OIDC

Format

XML-based

JSON Web Tokens (JWT)

Interoperability

Widely adopted with commercial and open-source providers

Rapid adoption across platforms

Complexity

More complicated, XML-based

Easier, JSON and REST-based

Attributes

Customizable attribute profiles

Standardized scopes and claims

Security

digital signatures and encryption

encryption

Use Case

Enterprises, educational institutions

Consumer apps, social logins, SPAs

 

SAP Context

In your  SAP landscape, both SAML and OIDC are supported with SAP Cloud Identity Services. Whether you’re working with SAP applications on-premises or in the cloud, you have the flexibility to choose either protocol. However, it's worth noting that more and more SAP solutions, like Task Center, are require OIDC to be able to interoperate between different applications (see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/migration-from-saml-trust-to-openid-connect-trust-with-identity-authentication)

 

What You Should Remember

  1. Similarity in Functionality: Both SAML and OIDC perform similar roles in SSO implementation, providing secure authentication and authorization.
  2. Support in SAP: Both protocols are supported by SAP applications using SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  3. OIDC for Lower Latency: OIDC offers lower latency, making it better suited for lightweight service authentication and service-to-service contexts.
  4. Reliability of SAML: If you’ve been using SAML in your architecture, rest assured it’s reliable and will likely remain a mainstay for SSO implementations in end-user applications.
  5. Future with OIDC: For new applications and service-to-service authentication, consider embracing OIDC due to its efficient and easier-to-implement nature.

Choosing the right protocol ultimately depends on your specific needs and existing infrastructure, but both SAML and OIDC provide robust and secure frameworks for SSO, ensuring seamless user experiences across your SAP landscape.

entry.title: Re: Emailing Sales order as PDF attachment when created in VA01

entry.title: Downloading IBM MQ 9.3 client libraries compatible with SAP PO 7.5
entry.content:
We like to get the right Client Libraries for IBM MQ 9.3 for SAP PI 7.5


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Downloading IBM MQ 9.3 client libraries compatible wi...
entry.content:
You can Download IBM MQ 9.3 libraries from IBM website or IBM Passport Advantage. I think the following link is the one you need: WebSphere, IBM MQ (9.3.0.0, All platforms) 
However I would note that from SAP Side we cannot comment more on  the most stable version of those libraries an the secure url to download them as this is a third party product. Therefore I recommend you to get in touch with your IBM MQ vendor for the same.
Regarding the compatibility with your PO system, you need just to ensure that the downloaded libraries are compatible with JRE 8 (build with JDK 😎 as your SAP PO 7.5 is running on JRE 8. You can find this in the IBM MQ documentation or ask for confirmation from IBM vendor.
For details on how to deploy the IBM MQ libraries and their compatibility with the PO 7.5 please follow the bellow SAP documentations:

  • Guided Answers: How to deploy the external drivers for Axis/JDBC/JMS Adapter
  • Point 3 of the resolution section of the SAP KBA: 1615198 - PI JDBC and JMS Adapter driver's JRE compatibility 
  • 747601 - PI JMS Adapter: IBM MQ client libraries

Bes Regards

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Emailing Sales order as PDF attachment when created in VA01
